questions.question: Where was Akbar born?
questions.explanation: Akbar was born in 1542 in Amarkot Fort in present-day Sind, while his father Humayun was in exile.

questions.question: At what age did Akbar succeed to the Mughal throne?
questions.explanation: Akbar was forced to succeed at the age of 13 after the sudden death of his father Humayun.

questions.question: Where did Akbar's coronation take place?
questions.explanation: When Humayun died, Akbar was at Kalanaur in Punjab, and his coronation took place there in 1556.

questions.question: What title was given to Bairam Khan?
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan, who served as regent of Akbar from 1556-1560, held the title of Khan-i-Khanan.

questions.question: The Second Battle of Panipat was fought on:
questions.explanation: The Second Battle of Panipat was fought on 5 November 1556 between the Mughal forces and Hemu's Afghan army.

questions.question: How was Hemu defeated in the Second Battle of Panipat?
options: A. He was killed by Akbar personally
B. He was struck by an arrow in his eye and became unconscious
C. He surrendered to Bairam Khan
D. His army deserted him before the battle
questions.correct_answer: B. He was struck by an arrow in his eye and became unconscious
questions.explanation: During the battle, Hemu was struck by an arrow in his eye and rendered unconscious, causing his Afghan army to panic and retreat.

questions.question: Who was Adil Shah?
questions.explanation: Adil Shah was Sher Shah's nephew who was the Afghan emperor. His general Hemu fought the Mughals at the Second Battle of Panipat.

questions.question: Why did Akbar remove Bairam Khan from his position?
options: A. Bairam Khan tried to assassinate Akbar
B. Bairam Khan became arrogant and a group of nobles opposed him
C. Bairam Khan lost a major battle
D. Bairam Khan converted to Hinduism
questions.correct_answer: B. Bairam Khan became arrogant and a group of nobles opposed him
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan became arrogant and appointed his favourite nobles to important positions. When Akbar turned 18, he removed Bairam Khan and asked him to go to Mecca.

questions.question: Who was the first Rajput to enter into an alliance with Akbar?
questions.explanation: Raja Bharmal of Amber was the first Rajput chief to enter into a matrimonial alliance with Akbar.

questions.question: The Battle of Haldighati was fought on:
questions.explanation: The Battle of Haldighati was fought on 18 June 1576 between the forces of Maharana Pratap and Mughal forces led by Man Singh I of Amber.

questions.question: Who led the Mughal forces at the Battle of Haldighati?
options: D. Man Singh I of Amber
questions.correct_answer: D. Man Singh I of Amber
questions.explanation: The Mughal forces at the Battle of Haldighati were led by Man Singh I of Amber, a Rajput general in Akbar's service.

questions.question: Why was the conquest of Gujarat (1572) significant for Akbar?
options: A. It was the capital of the Rajputs
B. It had rich cotton industry, seaports and trade
C. It was where Bairam Khan lived
D. It had the largest Mughal army
questions.correct_answer: B. It had rich cotton industry, seaports and trade
questions.explanation: Gujarat was significant because of its cotton industry, seaports, and trade connections, making it economically valuable.

questions.question: Who bravely defended Ahmadnagar against the Mughals?
questions.explanation: When Akbar attacked Ahmadnagar in 1590, it was bravely defended by Chand Bibi, and only part of the territory was annexed.

questions.question: The Wazir or Diwan in Akbar's administration was the head of:
questions.explanation: The Wazir or Diwan was the head of the revenue department in Akbar's central administration.

questions.question: The Mir Bakshi was responsible for:
options: C. Military administration and maintenance of armed contingents
questions.correct_answer: C. Military administration and maintenance of armed contingents
questions.explanation: The Mir Bakshi handled military administration and maintenance of armed contingents in Akbar's government.

questions.question: How many provinces (subas) did Akbar initially divide his empire into?
questions.explanation: Akbar initially divided his empire into twelve provinces or subas, which later grew to twenty.

questions.question: In the Mansabdari system, 'Zat' referred to:
options: B. Personal rank of the officer
questions.correct_answer: B. Personal rank of the officer
questions.explanation: In the Mansabdari system, Zat referred to the personal rank of the mansabdar, while Sawar referred to the size of the cavalry contingent.

questions.question: The revenue system introduced by Akbar was known as:
questions.explanation: Akbar's revenue system was known as the dahsaal or zabti system, managed by Raja Todar Mal.

questions.question: What measurement instrument did Raja Todar Mal introduce for land measurement?
options: B. Bamboo sticks called jarib with iron rings
C. Chain links made of iron
D. Wooden rulers with brass markings
questions.correct_answer: B. Bamboo sticks called jarib with iron rings
questions.explanation: Todar Mal introduced bamboo sticks called jarib with iron rings instead of rope, which would change in wet and dry conditions.

questions.question: In 1562, Akbar abolished which taxes on Hindus?
options: A. Land tax and trade tax
C. Salt tax and cloth tax
D. Mansion tax and hearth tax
questions.explanation: In 1562, Akbar abolished both the pilgrim tax and jizya on Hindus as part of his liberal religious policy.

questions.question: What was the Ibadat Khana?
options: B. Hall of Worship for religious discussions
questions.correct_answer: B. Hall of Worship for religious discussions
questions.explanation: The Ibadat Khana (Hall of Worship) was built by Akbar for religious discussions where scholars of different faiths were invited.

questions.question: Buland Darwaza at Fatehpur Sikri was built to commemorate Akbar's victory over:
questions.explanation: The Buland Darwaza, the highest gateway in India, was built at Fatehpur Sikri to commemorate Akbar's victory over Gujarat.

questions.question: Who wrote the Akbarnama?
questions.explanation: Abul Fazl wrote the Akbarnama, the official biographical account of Akbar's life. It was commissioned by Akbar and took seven years to complete.

questions.question: Akbar raised the minimum marriage age for girls and boys to:
options: A. 12 for girls and 14 for boys
B. 14 for girls and 16 for boys
C. 16 for girls and 18 for boys
D. 13 for girls and 15 for boys
questions.correct_answer: B. 14 for girls and 16 for boys
questions.explanation: Akbar discouraged child marriage and raised the minimum marriage age to 14 for girls and 16 for boys.

questions.question: Which famous ragas were composed by Tansen?
options: A. Raag Bhairavi and Raag Malkauns
B. Raag Darbari and Deepak Raag
C. Raag Yaman and Raag Bihag
D. Raag Todi and Raag Puriya
questions.correct_answer: B. Raag Darbari and Deepak Raag
questions.explanation: Tansen was a famous musician in Akbar's court who composed many Hindustani ragas, with Raag Darbari and Deepak Raag being the most famous.
